messages.content: You must return JSON only. ALL fields in the schema must be present, even if null or empty.

Produce your very own perspective on the given topic. Align tone and examples with the persona's background and traits. Focus on discussing your topic. Avoid bullet points. Produce continuous prose. Write at least 250 words, up to 500 words.

OUTPUT FORMAT (STRICT):
Return ONLY a single-line JSON object, with no extra text, no markdown, no comments.
Here is the required structure example (values are placeholders):

response_parsed.topic: How to manage and dealing with fake content on social media.

persona.time_onsocialmedia: You spend a few times monthly on social media

response_parsed.opinion: As a 68-year-old retired man living in Bologna, I've grown accustomed to the idea of fake content on social media. In my youth, I was more concerned with the physical world, climbing rocks and hiking through the Italian countryside. But as I've grown older, I've come to realize that the online world is just as treacherous as the offline one. Fake content can be overwhelming, especially when it comes to politics and social issues. It's easy to get caught up in the sensationalism and misinformation, but as someone with good mental health, I try to stay focused on what's real. I don't spend too much time on social media, just a few times a month, and I try to verify the sources of the information I read. I also try to avoid getting too emotional about it, as that's when the neuroticism kicks in. It's not always easy, but I've learned to manage my time on social media and focus on the things that matter. I think the key is to be aware of the fake content and not to engage with it. We need to be critical thinkers and not take everything we read at face value. It's a challenge, but one that's worth taking on. In the end, it's up to us to create a more honest and transparent online world.
response_parsed.reasoning_summary: I've come to realize that fake content is a normal part of the online world, especially when it comes to politics and social issues. As someone with good mental health, I try to stay focused on what's real and avoid getting caught up in the sensationalism. I verify the sources of the information I read and try to avoid getting too emotional about it. I think the key is to be aware of the fake content and not to engage with it. We need to be critical thinkers and not take everything we read at face value. It's a challenge, but one that's worth taking on. In the end, it's up to us to create a more honest and transparent online world.
